Description:
Keycards are short-range identification tokens used by Robotek facility doors, vehicle locks, and tier-locked machinery. Each card carries an embedded credential keyed to specific access tiers and, for higher security applications, specific facilities or specific zones within a facility.

The card system was originally introduced in 2371 as a replacement for biometric access, after a series of high-profile cases in which biometric scanners failed to distinguish between authorized personnel and units that had recently consumed them. Keycards solved the problem by storing identity in something a robot couldn't accidentally eat.

Cards are not copy-protected to a useful standard. The cryptographic standard used by most Robotek doors has been broken since 2398, but full system replacement was deemed economically unjustified. As a result, working keycards retain considerable value among parties who cannot otherwise pass through specific doors. They are also tradeable — though the practice of looting them from former owners has produced a robust secondary market, and a quieter primary one.

Each keycard recovered grants access only to whatever it was issued for. Collecting them is a matter of finding the right card for the right door.
